{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Support Officer",
  "description": "The International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA) is a not-for-profit institution that generates agricultural innovations to meet Africa&rsquo;s most pressing challenges of hunger, malnutrition, poverty, and natural resource degradation. Working with various partners across sub-Saharan Africa, we improve livelihoods, enhance food and nutrition security, increase employment, and preserve natural resource integrity. IITA is a member of CGIAR, a global agriculture research partnership for a food secure future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Support Officer

Job ID: STS2026-08
Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Duties


	Conduct assessment on community business opportunities tailored for youth, women, and vulnerable groups.
	Conduct market assessments to evaluate the financial viability of proposed agricultural enterprises.
	Conduct skills gap assessments for onboarded youth and women groups.
	Support program partners in organizing entrepreneurial and business training for youths at the nurseries and demonstration fields.
	Collaborate with program partners in the creation of youth and women led businesses in targeted rural communities.
	Provide field updates to assist in preparing project progress reports and strategic planning for agricultural initiatives.
	Guide young entrepreneurs in drafting basic agribusiness plans and value chain models.
	Conduct enterprise verification exercises to evaluate program compliance and impact.
	Perform any other job-related task as may be assigned by the Supervisor.


Qualifications


	BSc / HND in Agriculture or related field with a minimum of two (2) years of experience providing Agribusiness project or program support in a well-structured environment.


Competencies
The ideal candidate must:


	Have strong background in agriculture or agribusiness, with an entrepreneurial mind-set and the ability to identify and evaluate community-based business opportunities.
	Have basic financial literacy with the capacity to support youth and women in record keeping, business planning, and enterprise development.
	Have excellent communication, problem-solving, and innovative thinking skills, with the ability to work collaboratively in diverse teams and meet targets under pressure.
	Be detail-oriented, with the ability to work in varying weather conditions and undertake regular in-country travel.

Business Support Officer Job at the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)




        
    

        
        The International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA) is a not-for-profit institution that generates agricultural innovations to meet Africa’s most pressing challenges of hunger, malnutrition, poverty, and natural resource degradation. Working with various partners across sub-Saharan Africa, we improve livelihoods, enhance food and nutrition security, increase employment, and preserve natural resource integrity. IITA is a member of CGIAR, a global agriculture research partnership for a food secure future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Support Officer

Job ID: STS2026-08
Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Duties
     

            
    




	Conduct assessment on community business opportunities tailored for youth, women, and vulnerable groups.
	Conduct market assessments to evaluate the financial viability of proposed agricultural enterprises.
	Conduct skills gap assessments for onboarded youth and women groups.
	Support program partners in organizing entrepreneurial and business training for youths at the nurseries and demonstration fields.
	Collaborate with program partners in the creation of youth and women led businesses in targeted rural communities.
	Provide field updates to assist in preparing project progress reports and strategic planning for agricultural initiatives.
	Guide young entrepreneurs in drafting basic agribusiness plans and value chain models.
	Conduct enterprise verification exercises to evaluate program compliance and impact.
	Perform any other job-related task as may be assigned by the Supervisor.


Qualifications


	BSc / HND in Agriculture or related field with a minimum of two (2) years of experience providing Agribusiness project or program support in a well-structured environment.


Competencies
The ideal candidate must:


	Have strong background in agriculture or agribusiness, with an entrepreneurial mind-set and the ability to identify and evaluate community-based business opportunities.
	Have basic financial literacy with the capacity to support youth and women in record keeping, business planning, and enterprise development.
	Have excellent communication, problem-solving, and innovative thinking skills, with the ability to work collaboratively in diverse teams and meet targets under pressure.
	Be detail-oriented, with the ability to work in varying weather conditions and undertake regular in-country travel.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Export Manager (Manufacturing / FMCG / Logistics)",
  "description": "ShogxyPro Outsourcing is a forward-thinking HR and talent solutions firm based in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in recruitment, staff outsourcing, compliance management, and workforce development for businesses across various industries. Our goal is simple: help organizations grow by connecting them with the right people and building compliant, efficient HR systems. We serve startups, SMEs, and large enterprises looking to scale without the burden of in-house HR challenges.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Export Manager (Manufacturing / FMCG / Logistics)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly experienced and commercially driven Export Manager to lead and expand our international trade operations within a fast-paced manufacturing and FMCG environment supported by strong logistics operations.
	The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of export processes, global markets, and supply chain coordination.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute export strategies to drive international sales growth for manufactured/FMCG products.
	Identify and penetrate new international markets while strengthening existing distributor and partner networks.
	Manage the full export lifecycle, including order processing, documentation (Form M, PAAR, Bill of Lading, etc.), shipping, and customs clearance.
	Coordinate closely with production, warehouse, and logistics teams to ensure timely order fulfillment and efficient delivery schedules.
	Ensure compliance with international trade laws, customs regulations, and export documentation standards.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with international clients, distributors, freight forwarders, and regulatory bodies.
	Monitor inventory levels and align export demand with production planning to avoid stockouts or overproduction.
	Negotiate pricing, contracts, and payment terms (LCs, advance payments, etc.) with overseas clients.
	Track export performance, prepare reports, and provide market intelligence on global trends, pricing, and competitors.
	Optimize logistics and supply chain costs to improve efficiency and profitability.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, International Trade, Supply Chain, Marketing, or related field.
	10&ndash;15 years of experience in export management within manufacturing, FMCG, or logistics-driven organizations.
	Strong knowledge of export documentation, shipping procedures, INCOTERMS, and customs regulations.
	Hands-on experience working with freight forwarders, shipping lines, and clearing agents.
	Solid understanding of production planning and supply chain coordination.
	Proven ability to develop international markets and manage distributor relationships.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
	Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP) and Microsoft Office tools.
	Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret market and sales data.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: ibukun.fakoya@valencyafrica.com using &ldquo;Application for Export Manager Role&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

Export Manager (Manufacturing / FMCG / Logistics) Job at ShogxyPro Outsourcing




        
    

        
        ShogxyPro Outsourcing is a forward-thinking HR and talent solutions firm based in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in recruitment, staff outsourcing, compliance management, and workforce development for businesses across various industries. Our goal is simple: help organizations grow by connecting them with the right people and building compliant, efficient HR systems. We serve startups, SMEs, and large enterprises looking to scale without the burden of in-house HR challenges.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Export Manager (Manufacturing / FMCG / Logistics)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced and commercially driven Export Manager to lead and expand our international trade operations within a fast-paced manufacturing and FMCG environment supported by strong logistics operations.
	The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of export processes, global markets, and supply chain coordination.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute export strategies to drive international sales growth for manufactured/FMCG products.
	Identify and penetrate new international markets while strengthening existing distributor and partner networks.
	Manage the full export lifecycle, including order processing, documentation (Form M, PAAR, Bill of Lading, etc.), shipping, and customs clearance.
	Coordinate closely with production, warehouse, and logistics teams to ensure timely order fulfillment and efficient delivery schedules.
	Ensure compliance with international trade laws, customs regulations, and export documentation standards.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with international clients, distributors, freight forwarders, and regulatory bodies.
	Monitor inventory levels and align export demand with production planning to avoid stockouts or overproduction.
	Negotiate pricing, contracts, and payment terms (LCs, advance payments, etc.) with overseas clients.
	Track export performance, prepare reports, and provide market intelligence on global trends, pricing, and competitors.
	Optimize logistics and supply chain costs to improve efficiency and profitability.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, International Trade, Supply Chain, Marketing, or related field.
	10–15 years of experience in export management within manufacturing, FMCG, or logistics-driven organizations.
	Strong knowledge of export documentation, shipping procedures, INCOTERMS, and customs regulations.
	Hands-on experience working with freight forwarders, shipping lines, and clearing agents.
	Solid understanding of production planning and supply chain coordination.
	Proven ability to develop international markets and manage distributor relationships.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
	Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP) and Microsoft Office tools.
	Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret market and sales data.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using “Application for Export Manager Role” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Care Coordinator",
  "description": "Oxenhr Limited - We are a people-focused recruitment company dedicated to connecting top talent with reputable organizations across multiple industries. Our services include talent sourcing, screening, and placement for both permanent and contract roles. With a strong commitment to quality, integrity, and career growth, we help employers build high-performing teams while supporting candidates in finding roles that match their skills and ambitions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Care Coordinator

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	We are seeking an organised, compassionate, and proactive Care Coordinator to join our growing care team.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for coordinating care services, ensuring high-quality care delivery, supporting compliance with regulatory requirements, and maintaining effective communication with service users, families, and care staff.
	The Care Coordinator will play a key role in workforce planning, care package implementation, scheduling, documentation, and operational administration to ensure safe, effective, and person-centred care.


Key Responsibilities
Workforce Planning &amp; Rota Management:


	Prepare and maintain weekly and monthly staff rotas.
	Allocate care staff efficiently based on availability, skills, and service user needs.
	Ensure continuity of care by matching carers appropriately.
	Manage emergency cover and last-minute rota changes.


Competency &amp; Compliance Support:


	Monitor staff training and competency matrices.
	Ensure all care staff remain compliant with mandatory training and certifications.
	Update compliance records on the Care App and internal systems.
	Escalate expiring training or documentation to management.


Care Package Coordination:


	Coordinate the successful mobilisation of new care packages.
	Ensure all required documentation is completed before care commences.
	Support the delivery of a minimum of 24 successfully implemented care packages annually (or organisational target).


Business Development Support:


	Coordinate new service user referrals.
	Support the onboarding of new service users.
	Contribute towards increasing new commissioned care hours.


Care Planning &amp; Reviews:


	Create comprehensive service user profiles.
	Develop, update, and maintain person-centred care plans.
	Conduct scheduled care plan reviews.
	Ensure accurate documentation within the Care App.


Care Scheduling:


	Schedule all service user visits using the electronic Care App.
	Ensure visits are accurately assigned and monitored.
	Resolve scheduling conflicts promptly.


Risk Assessment Management:


	Complete and review risk assessments.
	Ensure all service user records remain current.
	Monitor compliance using the Care App and internal compliance matrix.


Supervision &amp; Appraisal Coordination:


	Schedule staff supervision meetings.
	Coordinate annual appraisals.
	Maintain accurate supervision and appraisal records.
	Monitor completion rates and report outstanding reviews.


Timesheet Administration:


	Review electronic and paper timesheets.
	Verify visit completion and attendance.
	Resolve discrepancies before payroll processing.
	Ensure accurate payment information is submitted.


Variance Reporting:


	Prepare weekly and monthly service user variance reports.
	Monitor commissioned versus delivered care hours.
	Submit variance reports within agreed deadlines.


Client Invoice Administration:


	Generate and maintain accurate client invoices.
	Track invoice status and resolve billing discrepancies.
	Liaise with finance regarding payment issues.


Medication Management:


	Prepare and maintain both electronic and paper Medication Administration Records (MAR Charts).
	Ensure medication documentation complies with regulatory requirements.
	Monitor medication record accuracy.


Service User Relationship Management:


	Maintain positive relationships with service users and their families.
	Obtain regular feedback regarding service quality.
	Record all feedback within the Care App.
	Escalate complaints or safeguarding concerns promptly.


Care Staff Performance Monitoring:


	Monitor individual care staff productivity.
	Produce monthly task and performance reports.
	Support managers in achieving operational performance targets.


Staff Onboarding &amp; Induction:


	Coordinate induction programmes for new care and administrative staff.
	Ensure completion of departmental orientation.
	Maintain onboarding records and compliance documentation.


Person Specification
Essential:


	Previous experience in domiciliary care coordination or healthcare administration.
	Excellent organisational and planning skills.
	Experience using electronic care management systems.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good understanding of care planning and risk assessment.
	Ability to prioritise workload and work under pressure.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Competent in Microsoft Office applications.
	Knowledge of safeguarding, medication management, and care compliance.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent planning and coordination skills.
	Strong problem-solving ability.
	Customer-focused approach.
	Leadership and team support skills.
	Excellent record-keeping and documentation.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
	Professionalism and confidentiality.
	Time management and multitasking.
	High attention to compliance and quality standards.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary: N250,000 Gross
	Pension scheme.
	HMO
	Exceptional Reward on New Care Package brought.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitments@oxenhr.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Care Coordinator Job at Oxenhr Limited




        
    

        
        Oxenhr Limited - We are a people-focused recruitment company dedicated to connecting top talent with reputable organizations across multiple industries. Our services include talent sourcing, screening, and placement for both permanent and contract roles. With a strong commitment to quality, integrity, and career growth, we help employers build high-performing teams while supporting candidates in finding roles that match their skills and ambitions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Care Coordinator

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking an organised, compassionate, and proactive Care Coordinator to join our growing care team.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for coordinating care services, ensuring high-quality care delivery, supporting compliance with regulatory requirements, and maintaining effective communication with service users, families, and care staff.
	The Care Coordinator will play a key role in workforce planning, care package implementation, scheduling, documentation, and operational administration to ensure safe, effective, and person-centred care.


Key Responsibilities
Workforce Planning & Rota Management:


	Prepare and maintain weekly and monthly staff rotas.
	Allocate care staff efficiently based on availability, skills, and service user needs.
	Ensure continuity of care by matching carers appropriately.
	Manage emergency cover and last-minute rota changes.


Competency & Compliance Support:


	Monitor staff training and competency matrices.
	Ensure all care staff remain compliant with mandatory training and certifications.
	Update compliance records on the Care App and internal systems.
	Escalate expiring training or documentation to management.


Care Package Coordination:


	Coordinate the successful mobilisation of new care packages.
	Ensure all required documentation is completed before care commences.
	Support the delivery of a minimum of 24 successfully implemented care packages annually (or organisational target).


Business Development Support:


	Coordinate new service user referrals.
	Support the onboarding of new service users.
	Contribute towards increasing new commissioned care hours.


Care Planning & Reviews:


	Create comprehensive service user profiles.
	Develop, update, and maintain person-centred care plans.
	Conduct scheduled care plan reviews.
	Ensure accurate documentation within the Care App.


Care Scheduling:


	Schedule all service user visits using the electronic Care App.
	Ensure visits are accurately assigned and monitored.
	Resolve scheduling conflicts promptly.


Risk Assessment Management:


	Complete and review risk assessments.
	Ensure all service user records remain current.
	Monitor compliance using the Care App and internal compliance matrix.


Supervision & Appraisal Coordination:


	Schedule staff supervision meetings.
	Coordinate annual appraisals.
	Maintain accurate supervision and appraisal records.
	Monitor completion rates and report outstanding reviews.


Timesheet Administration:


	Review electronic and paper timesheets.
	Verify visit completion and attendance.
	Resolve discrepancies before payroll processing.
	Ensure accurate payment information is submitted.


Variance Reporting:


	Prepare weekly and monthly service user variance reports.
	Monitor commissioned versus delivered care hours.
	Submit variance reports within agreed deadlines.


Client Invoice Administration:


	Generate and maintain accurate client invoices.
	Track invoice status and resolve billing discrepancies.
	Liaise with finance regarding payment issues.


Medication Management:


	Prepare and maintain both electronic and paper Medication Administration Records (MAR Charts).
	Ensure medication documentation complies with regulatory requirements.
	Monitor medication record accuracy.


Service User Relationship Management:


	Maintain positive relationships with service users and their families.
	Obtain regular feedback regarding service quality.
	Record all feedback within the Care App.
	Escalate complaints or safeguarding concerns promptly.


Care Staff Performance Monitoring:


	Monitor individual care staff productivity.
	Produce monthly task and performance reports.
	Support managers in achieving operational performance targets.


Staff Onboarding & Induction:


	Coordinate induction programmes for new care and administrative staff.
	Ensure completion of departmental orientation.
	Maintain onboarding records and compliance documentation.


Person Specification
Essential:


	Previous experience in domiciliary care coordination or healthcare administration.
	Excellent organisational and planning skills.
	Experience using electronic care management systems.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good understanding of care planning and risk assessment.
	Ability to prioritise workload and work under pressure.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Competent in Microsoft Office applications.
	Knowledge of safeguarding, medication management, and care compliance.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent planning and coordination skills.
	Strong problem-solving ability.
	Customer-focused approach.
	Leadership and team support skills.
	Excellent record-keeping and documentation.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
	Professionalism and confidentiality.
	Time management and multitasking.
	High attention to compliance and quality standards.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary: N250,000 Gross
	Pension scheme.
	HMO
	Exceptional Reward on New Care Package brought.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Administrative and Customer Care Executive Job at Lumira Advisory




        
    

        
        Lumira Advisory - Our client, Magna Eye Care, is a modern eye clinic located at No. 2, Ado-Ekiti Closeoff Emeka Anyaoku Street, Area 11, Garki, Abuja, F.C.T. They are committed to delivering exceptional eye care with excellence,with a strong focus on patient experience. Join their team and be part of a growing community that values excellence, professionalism, and care, creating a warm and memorable healthcare experience.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative and Customer Care Executive
     

            
    



Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
Working Hours: 5 days weekly from 8 am - 5:30pm
Resumption: The applicants should be available to resume on the 6th of July 2026.

Role Overview


	We are seeking a vibrant, intelligent, pleasant, and creative individual to represent the face and voice of Magna Eye Care.
	This role involves you carry out daily operational activities to meet theneeds of our esteemed patients.


Responsibilities


	You will be expected to perform the following tasks to the highest. Greet and attend to patients with warmth and professionalism.
	Handle phone calls, WhatsApp, emails, and patient queries.
	Schedule appointments and manage daily bookings.
	Maintain accurate patient records and support filing systems.
	Coordinate administrative duties to support clinic efficiency.
	Assist with patient flow and overall clinic coordination, ensuringthey have a pleasant experience.
	Assist patients to get their treatments, which include frames,lenses and drugs.
	Support doctors and clinical staff with documentation or tasks.
	Keep proper documentation of patients' transactions, stocks, etc.
	Provide clear information about products, services, andappointments.
	Maintain a warm, respectful, and solutions-focused approach in all communications.
	Address patient inquiries and concerns with empathy, accuracy, and speed.
	Maintain a high standard of communication and representationfor the clinic.
	Participate in clinic-wide communication and team meetings.
	Implement growth strategies focused on brand awareness.
	Follow up with patients with booked appointments and patientswho have not concluded their transactions.


Requirements


	BSc / HND
	Stable work history
	Experience in customer care, sales, marketing and customer care is an advantage but not mandatory.


Key Qualities & Skills:


	Excellent communication skills (written and spoken English).
	Emotional intelligence and interpersonal skills.
	Warm, respectful, and service-oriented.
	Highly organised with good attention to detail.
	Ability to multitask and work independently.
	Basic computer and digital literacy.
	A team player with a positive, can-do attitude.
	Excellent time management and pleasant, creative, and professional personality.
	Strong time management and organisation skills.
	Passion for customer care and creating great patient experiences.
	Ability to influence sales.
	Demonstrate the highest level of ethical behaviour.
	Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
	Critical thinking skills and high emotional intelligence skills.
	Proven organisation management skills with meticulous attention to detail.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc).


Remuneration


	Salary: N200,000 gross monthly with additional sales bonuses.
	Tax and pension deductions will be applied.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only candidates who reside in Abuja are invited to apply.
